pr.title: Ignore VS Code's files properly and fix `.clang-format` to be proper YAML
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Repository hygiene PR that replaced the root `.clang-format` inline map with a proper YAML-style clang-format file, changed `.gitignore` so VS Code project files are selectively ignored instead of ignoring the whole `.vscode/` directory, and added a committed `.vscode/settings.json` carrying the C/C++ extension clang-format style.
what_changed: Converted `.clang-format` from a single braced inline YAML map to ordinary key/value YAML entries, keeping the same clang-format options such as `BasedOnStyle: LLVM`, `PointerAlignment: Left`, `IndentWidth: 4`, `ColumnLimit: 0`, and custom brace wrapping.
Removed the blanket `.vscode/` ignore rule from `.gitignore`.

Added VS Code ignore rules based on GitHub's Global/VisualStudioCode.gitignore: ignore `.vscode/*` by default, but allow committed workspace files such as `.vscode/settings.json`, `tasks.json`, `launch.json`, `extensions.json`, and `.vscode/*.code-snippets`.
Added ignores for VS Code `.history/` and built extension packages `*.vsix`.
Added `.vscode/settings.json` with `C_Cpp.clang_format_style` set to an inline clang-format style matching the repository's formatting choices.
smart_moves: Selective `.vscode` ignores let the project commit shared editor settings while keeping local workspace clutter out of git.
Committing a VS Code C/C++ clang-format style helps VS Code users apply the same formatting conventions without relying only on their personal editor setup.
Referencing GitHub's published VisualStudioCode.gitignore pattern makes the ignore policy recognizable and maintainable.
Changing `.clang-format` into conventional YAML improves tool compatibility and readability while preserving the intended formatting policy.
decomp_lessons: Shared formatting/editor configuration is useful decomp infrastructure: consistent whitespace and brace style reduce noisy diffs in matching work.
Do not ignore an entire editor configuration directory if some files inside it are meant to be shared by the project; use negated `.gitignore` rules for the allowed files.
Tooling fixes can matter even when no game code changes: a valid `.clang-format` and editor integration help contributors avoid accidental style churn.
naming_conventions: VS Code shared configuration lives under `.vscode/settings.json`.
The committed VS Code setting key used for formatting is `C_Cpp.clang_format_style`.

The root clang-format configuration is named `.clang-format` and uses clang-format option names such as `BasedOnStyle`, `DerivePointerAlignment`, `PointerAlignment`, `UseTab`, `IndentWidth`, and `ColumnLimit`.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that modify `.clang-format` or formatting policy.
Find PRs that add or update `.vscode` shared editor settings.
Find repository hygiene PRs that adjust `.gitignore` for editor-generated files.
